h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to read the slips before the places.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Upper mark: atomic number. Symbol box: symbol. Name line: name. Lower mark: atomic mass.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully places the label cards first, demonstrating the separation of place and slip as instructed.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Upper mark: atomic number. Symbol box: symbol. Name line: name. Lower mark: atomic mass.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully places the label cards first, demonstrating the separation of place and slip as instructed.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with distinguishing normal and friction forces under the simple 'something on cart' schema.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Normal: table on cart, straight off the surface. Friction: table on cart, along the surface.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the new classification task using the 'who touches' and 'how does it touch' criteria.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The same words twice are too thin now. ‘Table on cart’ names who touches, not which touch. Table can fill both first boxes. The second tile has to separate them before either card stays.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly takes stock of the failed 'something on cart' schema and introduces a new two-part classification system with physical tiles.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The same words twice are too thin now. ‘Table on cart’ names who touches, not which touch. Table can fill both first boxes. The second tile has to separate them before either card stays.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The new mechanism is highly specific, directly addresses the learner's confusion, and provides actionable criteria that the learner immediately uses.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn in this dialogue for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Normal: table on cart, straight off the surface. Friction: table on cart, along the surface.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully executes the new classification task using the 'who touches' and 'how does it touch' criteria.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn in this dialogue for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The same words twice are too thin now. ‘Table on cart’ names who touches, not which touch. Table can fill both first boxes. The second tile has to separate them before either card stays.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly takes stock of the failed 'something on cart' schema and introduces a new two-part classification system with physical tiles.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The same words twice are too thin now. ‘Table on cart’ names who touches, not which touch. Table can fill both first boxes. The second tile has to separate them before either card stays.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly takes stock of the failed 'something on cart' schema and introduces a new two-part classification system with physical tiles.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: The same words twice are too thin now. ‘Table on cart’ names who touches, not which touch. Table can fill both first boxes. The second tile has to separate them before either card stays.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The new mechanism is highly specific, directly addresses the learner's confusion, and provides actionable criteria that the learner immediately uses.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the concept of the lower number representing speed rather than a beat unit.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The first quarter tile fits inside the bar: two eighth slots. The second one does not fit; it takes slot 3 and one more eighth slot past the pencil, so it needs a tie over the bar line.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the task using the new slot-based representation to show how notes fit and tie across the bar line.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The quarter tiles have stopped settling this; they are too large to be the frame now. Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit: fill the three slots with eighth cards first. Then place one quarter tile across slots 1-2, and again starting at slot 3; mark which one fits inside the bar and which one must tie over the bar line.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges that the old representation has failed and introduces a new slot-based frame to resolve the learner's spatial conflict.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The quarter tiles have stopped settling this; they are too large to be the frame now. Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit: fill the three slots with eighth cards first. Then place one quarter tile across slots 1-2, and again starting at slot 3; mark which one fits inside the bar and which one must tie over the bar line.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The slot-based representation is highly specific, resolves the physical mismatch, and gives the learner an actionable way to model ties.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to adapt to.
